heroHeadline: UN Warns 'World Is Failing Sudan' on Third War Anniversary; 34M Need Aid as Amnesty Documents 700+ Civilians Killed in Drone Strikes in Q1 2026
heroSubtitle: UN Emergency Relief Coordinator Tom Fletcher marked Sudan's third civil war anniversary (April 22) with a stark warning that 'another year has passed when the world has failed to meet the test of Sudan': nearly 34 million people (65% of the population) require urgent humanitarian assistance, only ~16% of the $3B UN 2026 appeal is funded, and famine is confirmed in multiple areas; Amnesty International simultaneously documented three years of intensifying attacks on civilians including nearly 700 civilians killed in drone strikes in Q1 2026 alone (245 children) and systematic sexual violence in Darfur; an African Union delegation visited Sudan's Foreign Ministry to assess reopening the AU Khartoum office; SAF holds Blue Nile gains (Muqja recaptured April 21) while the Kordofan ground offensive continues
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon, Foreign Policy, The Reporter Ethiopia, RT Africa, Abren, Pravda Sudan, Radio Tamazuj, Amnesty International, Africanews, Wikipedia (Sudan Civil War Timeline 2026), NBC News, Middle East Eye, Deutsche Welle, Daily Pakistan, The Star (Reuters), AllAfrica, Soufan Center
